weather_report:
Subiaco wakes under a quilt of broken cloud, the sun sliding through in soft, polished bands that brighten the streets then retreat. The light is gentle rather than glaring, lending a clear, well-drawn look to the morning without the sharp dazzle of a blue-sky day.

Through late morning into the early afternoon, expect this on-and-off rhythm to continue: bright intervals tempered by drifting cloud. It won’t feel bleak, just pleasantly moderated; even so, the UV around midday is likely to be moderate to high despite the cloud breaks, so a hat, sunglasses, and a touch of sunscreen are wise. Light layers and breathable fabrics suit the mood. It’s a good day for unhurried errands on Rokeby Road, a quiet coffee by a window, or pottering in the garden while the shade comes and goes. If you’re out near noon, keep to the shadier side of the street and carry some water; let the softened sunlight do the rest.
